document.source_title: One Nation reveals gas policy – as it happened
document.summary: On May 21, 2026, One Nation announced a new resources policy inspired by Norway, proposing a 30% equity stake in offshore gas projects. Queensland's Olympics minister, Tim Mander, resigned amid a police investigation. Australia also saw a rise in unemployment to 4.5% in April. Penny Wong condemned the actions of an Israeli minister who mocked activists, while health officials reported a diphtheria outbreak in the APY Lands.
key_takeaways: One Nation proposes a Norway-inspired gas policy with a 30% equity stake in new ventures.
Tim Mander resigned from his cabinet position due to a police investigation.
Australia's unemployment rate rose to 4.5% in April.
Penny Wong condemned an Israeli minister's treatment of activists.
A diphtheria outbreak has been confirmed in South Australia's APY Lands.
The federal health minister announced a $7.2 million package to address the outbreak.
document.why_it_matters: These developments highlight significant political shifts in Australia, including policy changes in resource management, public health challenges, and international relations, reflecting the government's response to both domestic and global issues.

claims.claim_text: One Nation announced a Norway-inspired gas policy.
claims.evidence_text: One Nation announced its new resources policy; a Norway-inspired government equity scheme in prospective offshore oil and gas projects.

claims.claim_text: Queensland's Olympics minister resigned amid a police investigation.
claims.evidence_text: Queensland’s Olympics minister, Tim Mander, stood down from his cabinet position after being referred to the federal police over claims he may have committed a 'potential criminal offence'.
Australia recorded a surprise jump in unemployment, with the jobless rate lifting to 4.5% in April.

claims.claim_text: A diphtheria outbreak has been confirmed in the APY Lands.
claims.evidence_text: South Australia’s chief public health officer, Nicola Spurrier, has confirmed a number of people have tested positive to diphtheria in the state’s Aṉangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara (APY) Lands.
